idea.hook: What This Solar Flare Footage Is Showing
facts: A solar flare is an intense burst of radiation released when magnetic energy near sunspots erupts.
Solar observatories like SDO, SOHO, and STEREO watch active regions from different viewpoints.
idea.payoff: A solar flare is an intense burst of radiation released when magnetic energy near sunspots erupts.

upload.title: What This Solar Flare Footage Is Showing
upload.description:
This is real solar-observatory footage. The Sun is often shown in special wavelengths so flares and magnetic activity become visible.

A solar flare is an intense burst of radiation released when magnetic energy near sunspots erupts.

Source clip: Cosmic Origins Spectrograph: Large Scale Structure of the Universe
NASA source page: https://images.nasa.gov/details/GSFC_20080520_HST_m10223_COS
Uses NASA source media and public information. Do not imply NASA endorsed the finished video or AI output.

leader_kit.pinnedComment: A solar flare is an intense burst of radiation released when magnetic energy near sunspots erupts. What should this lesson explain next?
replyTemplates: The bright flash is not fire in the normal sense; it is radiation from magnetic energy erupting in the Sun's atmosphere.
Solar footage gets weird because spacecraft are often viewing the Sun in wavelengths our eyes cannot normally see.
leader_kit.disclosure: Uses NASA source media and public information. Do not imply NASA endorsed the finished video or AI output.

posting_decision.reason: Performance and timing both support an extra post today.
